work_unit: SP-SRV-M-002: cache loading thundering herd fix
situation: routes.js getPredictions/grid/contour endpoints use fixed setTimeout (2s/3s/5s) when cache.isLoading, causing concurrent requests to each wait independently then retry simultaneously (thundering herd). Under burst load this multiplies backend fetches instead of coalescing them.
action: Added cache.getOrLoad(key, loader, ttlMs) method to TTLCache that maintains an inflight promise map. Concurrent callers for the same key share a single loader promise. Replaced all three setTimeout-based loading patterns in routes.js with cache.getOrLoad calls.
outcome_evidence: Commit 9f7a9ae on auto/opt-routes-1783141439 (2 files changed, 90 insertions, 85 deletions). Both files pass node --check syntax validation. Pushed to origin.
next_suggestion: Next pending fix: SP-SRV-M-005 (routes.js 502 responses leak err.message to client) — remove detail field from 502 error responses in grid/contour/predictions routes.

audit_findings.finding: getPredictions uses setTimeout(2000) + single retry when cache.isLoading — N concurrent requests each wait 2s then all fire fetchAllCityData simultaneously (thundering herd)
/grid/:name uses setTimeout(3000) + single retry — same thundering herd pattern
/contour uses setTimeout(5000) + single retry — same thundering herd pattern
